Module com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java
Package com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.internal.util

Class ReentrantStampedLock

java.lang.Object
com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.internal.util.ReentrantStampedLock
public final class ReentrantStampedLock extends Object
A reentrant implementation of a StampedLock that can be used with try-with-resources.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    ReentrantStampedLock()
    Creates a new lock.

    Acquires a read-lock, unless the caller already holds a read or write-lock.

  • Constructor Details

    • ReentrantStampedLock

      public ReentrantStampedLock()
      Creates a new lock.

  • Method Details

    • optimisticRead

      public <V> V optimisticRead(Callable<V> task)
      Acquires an optimistic read-lock and runs a task. If an optimistic read-lock cannot be acquired, invokes readLock(task) instead.

      task is guaranteed to be invoked at least once, but must be safe to invoke multiple times as well. The return value must correspond to a local copy of the fields being read as there is no guarantee that state won't change between the time the lock is released and the time that the value is returned.

      Type Parameters:
      V - the type of value returned by the task
      Parameters:
      task - the task to run while holding the lock
      Returns:
      the value returned by the task
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if task is null
      WrappedCheckedException - if any checked exceptions are thrown

    • optimisticRead

      public void optimisticRead(WrappedCheckedException.CallableWithoutReturnValue task)
      Acquires an optimistic read-lock and runs a task. If an optimistic read-lock cannot be acquired, invokes readLock(task) instead.

      task is guaranteed to be invoked at least once, but must be safe to invoke multiple times as well. The return value must correspond to a local copy of the fields being read as there is no guarantee that state won't change between the time the lock is released and the time that the value is returned.

      Parameters:
      task - the task to run while holding the lock
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if task is null
      WrappedCheckedException - if any checked exceptions are thrown

    • read

      public CloseableLock read()
      Acquires a read-lock, unless the caller already holds a read or write-lock. If the caller already holds a lock, no lock is acquired or released.
      Returns:
      a read-lock as a resource

    • read

      public <V> V read(Callable<V> task)
      Runs a task while holding a read-lock. If the caller already holds a read or write-lock, no lock is acquired or released.
      Type Parameters:
      V - the type of value returned by the task
      Parameters:
      task - the task to run while holding the lock
      Returns:
      the value returned by the task
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if task is null
      WrappedCheckedException - if task throws a checked exception

    • read

      public void read(WrappedCheckedException.CallableWithoutReturnValue task)
      Runs a task while holding a read-lock. If the caller already holds a read or write-lock, no lock is acquired or released.
      Parameters:
      task - the task to run while holding the lock
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if task is null
      WrappedCheckedException - if task throws a checked exception

    • write

      public CloseableLock write()
      Acquires a write-lock, unless the caller already holds one. If the caller already holds a lock, no lock is acquired or released.

      If the caller holds a read-lock, an attempt is made to convert it into a write-lock. Conversions are not guaranteed to be atomic; consequently, there is no guarantee that state won't change between the time the read-lock is released and the write-lock is acquired. Callers must repeat any state checks to ensure that they still hold.

      Returns:
      a write-lock as a resource

    • write

      public <V> V write(Callable<V> task)
      Runs a task while holding a write-lock. If the caller already holds a lock, no lock is acquired or released.

      If the caller holds a read-lock, an attempt is made to convert it into a write-lock. Conversions are not guaranteed to be atomic; consequently, there is no guarantee that state won't change between the time the read-lock is released and the write-lock is acquired. Callers must repeat any state checks to ensure that they still hold.

      Type Parameters:
      V - the type of value returned by the task
      Parameters:
      task - the task to run while holding the lock
      Returns:
      the value returned by the task
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if task is null
      WrappedCheckedException - if task throws a checked exception

    • write

      public void write(WrappedCheckedException.CallableWithoutReturnValue task)
      Runs a task while holding a write-lock. If the caller already holds a lock, no lock is acquired or released.

      If the caller holds a read-lock, an attempt is made to convert it into a write-lock. Conversions are not guaranteed to be atomic; consequently, there is no guarantee that state won't change between the time the read-lock is released and the write-lock is acquired. Callers must repeat any state checks to ensure that they still hold.

      Parameters:
      task - the task to run while holding the lock
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if task is null
      WrappedCheckedException - if task throws a checked exception
